exercises.question: Mark the letter A, B, C or D to indicate the word whose underlined part differs from the other three in pronunciation: customer, truck, include, custard
exercises.explanation: 'include' has /uː/ sound, while A, B, D have /ʌ/ sound.

exercises.question: Mark the letter A, B, C or D to indicate the word whose underlined part differs from the other three in pronunciation: change, chemistry, choose, cheese
exercises.explanation: 'chemistry' has /k/ sound for 'ch', while A, C, D have /tʃ/ sound.

exercises.question: Mark the letter A, B, C or D to indicate the word that differs from the other three in the position of primary stress: comment, command, compose, machine
exercises.explanation: 'comment' has stress on the first syllable; B, C, D are stressed on the second.

exercises.question: Mark the letter A, B, C or D to indicate the word that differs from the other three in the position of primary stress: innovation, separation, installation, environment
exercises.explanation: 'environment' has stress on the second syllable; A, B, C have stress on the third.

exercises.question: It was an extremely frightened experience in my life. (Find the underlined part that needs correction)
exercises.explanation: Should be 'frightening' (V-ing adjective describes the experience, not a person).

exercises.question: I know you're too busy to stay, but I look forward to see you again. (Find the underlined part that needs correction)
exercises.explanation: 'look forward to' + V-ing. Should be 'to seeing'.

exercises.question: My aunt has begun taught English for twenty years. (Find the underlined part that needs correction)
exercises.explanation: Should be 'has been teaching' (present perfect continuous for duration).

exercises.question: I'm hungry, I _______ anything since 8 am.
exercises.explanation: Present perfect with 'since': I + haven't eaten. 'eaten' is the correct past participle.

exercises.question: He said that he was coming _______.
exercises.explanation: Reported speech time changes (Grammar Rule: backshifting): 'tomorrow' -> 'the next day/the following day', 'today' -> 'that day', 'yesterday' -> 'the day before/the previous day', 'now' -> 'then', 'here' -> 'there'.

exercises.question: Help me, _______ ?
exercises.explanation: Tag question for imperative: 'will you?' is used after requests.

exercises.question: If there were flying saucers, there _______ traces of their landing.
exercises.explanation: Conditional type 2: If + past simple, would + V-infinitive.

exercises.question: If you take a train or bus, you must pay a _______.
exercises.explanation: 'fare' is the money paid for public transport (bus, train, taxi).

exercises.question: If we don't _______ on electricity, there will be power cut.
exercises.explanation: 'economize on' = save/reduce spending on. A verb is needed after 'don't'.

exercises.question: My uncle _______ Paris two or three times.
exercises.explanation: Present perfect 'has visited' is used for experiences (two or three times).

exercises.question: He is a person _______ friends trust him.
exercises.explanation: 'whose' shows possession: whose friends = his friends.

exercises.question: If only I _______ a flower, I'd be a sunflower.
exercises.explanation: 'If only' + past subjunctive: 'were' is used for hypothetical situations.

exercises.question: Laura: "Have a good holiday!" Tim: "_______"
exercises.explanation: 'You too' is the natural response to wish someone the same.

exercises.question: Waiter: "Would you like a drink?" Customer: "_______"
exercises.explanation: 'Coffee, please' is a polite, direct response to ordering.

exercises.question: I want to hear the news. Can you switch on the radio?
exercises.explanation: 'switch on' = 'turn on' (activate a device).

exercises.question: Ralph Nader was the most prominent leader of the U.S consumer protection movement.
exercises.explanation: 'prominent' means important, well-known = 'significant'.

exercises.question: Fruit and vegetables grew in abundance on the island. The islanders even exported the surplus.
exercises.explanation: 'abundance' means a very large quantity. The opposite is 'small quantity'.

exercises.question: His conclusion is congruous with one previously drawn by Mayer.
exercises.explanation: 'congruous' means suitable, fitting. The opposite is 'unsuitable'.

exercises.passage: Garbage is a serious problem in U.S. Every year, Americans produce about 308 billion pounds. A lot of the garbage goes (23) _______ the landfills. But many landfills do not have room (24) _______ all the garbage. One answer to the garbage problem is recycling. Half of all the garbage in the U.S can be (25) _______ and use again. Recycling helps us solve the landfills problem and (26) _______ enough energy too. When people recycle newspapers, they save (27) _______ when they recycle glass, plastic, or cans, they save oil and electricity. Recycling is good for everyone.

questions.question: Fill blank (24)
questions.explanation: 'have room for' = have space for something.

questions.question: Fill blank (25)
questions.explanation: 'recycled and use again' - the context is about recycling garbage.

questions.question: Fill blank (27)
questions.explanation: Recycling newspapers saves trees (paper comes from trees).

exercises.passage: In the United States, people celebrate Mother's Day and Father's Day. Mother's Day is celebrated on the second Sunday in May. On this occasion, mother usually receives greeting cards and gifts from her husband and children. The best gift of all for an American Mom is a day of leisure. The majority of American mothers have outside jobs as well as housework, so their working days are often very hard. The working mother enjoys the traditional Mother's Day custom of breakfast (or brunch) cooked by her family and served to her on a tray in bed. Later in the day, it is also traditional for the extended family group to get together for dinner, either in a restaurant or in one of their homes. Flowers are an important part of Mother's Day. Mothers are often given a corsage or plant for the occasion, particularly if they are elderly. Father's Day is celebrated throughout the United States and Canada on the third Sunday in June. The holiday customs are similar to Mother's Day. Dad also receives greeting cards and gifts from his family and enjoys a day of leisure.

questions.question: Where do people celebrate Father's Day and Mother's Day?
questions.explanation: The passage begins: 'In the United States, people celebrate...'

questions.question: When is Mother's Day celebrated?
options: A. On the second Sunday in May
B. On the second of October
C. On the third Sunday in June
D. On the eight of March
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'Mother's Day is celebrated on the second Sunday in May.'

questions.question: Do the majority of American mothers have to work hard every day?
options: A. No, they only do the housework.
C. No, they don't have a job.
questions.explanation: The passage says 'their working days are often very hard.'

questions.question: What is an important part of Mother's Day?
questions.explanation: The passage explicitly states 'Flowers are an important part of Mother's Day.' Only 'flower' is specifically called 'an important part' in the text.

questions.question: When is Father's day celebrated?
options: A. On the second Sunday in May.
B. On the second of October.
C. On the third Sunday in June.
D. On the eight of March.
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'Father's Day is celebrated... on the third Sunday in June.'

exercises.original: "You should give up smoking as soon as possible", the doctor said to me.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with: The doctor advised me
exercises.answer: The doctor advised me to give up smoking as soon as possible.
acceptedAnswers: The doctor advised me to give up smoking as soon as possible.
The doctor advised me to give up smoking as soon as possible
The doctor advised me to stop smoking as soon as possible.
The doctor advised me to stop smoking as soon as possible
exercises.explanation: Reported advice: advise + object + to-infinitive. 'You should...' -> 'advised me to...'

exercises.original: People said that he grew a lot of cabbages in his field.
exercises.answer: He was said to grow a lot of cabbages in his field.
acceptedAnswers: He was said to grow a lot of cabbages in his field.
He was said to grow a lot of cabbages in his field
He was said to have grown a lot of cabbages in his field.
He was said to have grown a lot of cabbages in his field
exercises.explanation: Passive reporting: S + be + said + to-infinitive. When both verbs are past tense, 'to have V3' is also accepted.

exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with: It is difficult
exercises.answer: It is difficult to follow your directions.
acceptedAnswers: It is difficult to follow your directions.
It is difficult to follow your directions
exercises.explanation: V-ing + is + adj = It is + adj + to-infinitive.

exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with: He is interested
exercises.answer: He is interested in swimming and sunbathing.
acceptedAnswers: He is interested in swimming and sunbathing.
He is interested in swimming and sunbathing
exercises.explanation: 'be interested in' + V-ing = like doing something.

exercises.original: The professor is excellent. I am taking his course.
exercises.answer: The professor whose course I am taking is excellent.
acceptedAnswers: The professor whose course I am taking is excellent.
The professor whose course I am taking is excellent
exercises.explanation: 'whose' replaces 'his' to form a relative clause showing possession.

exercises.original: Mr. Tibbs doesn't drive carefully. Mr Smith doesn't drive carefully, either.
exercises.answer: Neither Mr. Tibbs nor Mr Smith drives carefully.
acceptedAnswers: Neither Mr. Tibbs nor Mr Smith drives carefully.
Neither Mr. Tibbs nor Mr Smith drives carefully
Neither Mr Tibbs nor Mr Smith drives carefully.
exercises.explanation: 'Neither... nor...' combines two negative ideas.

exercises.original: The shoes are too big. My mother can't fit her feet.
exercises.answer: The shoes are so big that my mother can't fit her feet.
acceptedAnswers: The shoes are so big that my mother can't fit her feet.
The shoes are so big that my mother can't fit her feet
The shoes are so big that my mother cannot fit her feet.
The shoes are so big that my mother cannot fit her feet
exercises.explanation: 'so + adjective + that' clause expresses result.

exercises.original: Ted has won a medal. Tony has won a medal, too.
exercises.answer: Both Ted and Tony have won a medal.
acceptedAnswers: Both Ted and Tony have won a medal.
Both Ted and Tony have won a medal
Both Ted and Tony have won medals.
exercises.explanation: 'Both... and...' combines two similar positive ideas.
